_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Strategy involves creating a plan to achieve a team or organisation's objectives.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to synthesize market trends, competitive intelligence, technology evolution, and organizational capability into coherent strategic recommendations that inform executive decision-making.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-A-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-008 (LS, cosine: 0.484), DG-S-009 (DG, cosine: 0.473).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.0/10 (raw 0.298). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DDaT-only terms [achieve, creating, involves, objectives, organisation, plan] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, capability, coherent, competitive, decision, evolution] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.864 (P=0.877, R=0.850). P exceeds R by 0.027 — the DDaT element's 12 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DDaT element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.2%). "Strategy involves creating a plan to achieve a team or organisation's objectives" and "Ability to synthesize market trends, competitive intelligence, technology evolut" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.486 > STS 0.298 (gap +0.188). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.1%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DDaT terms: [achieve, creating, involves, objectives, organisation, plan]. WIDAI terms: [ability, capability, coherent, competitive, decision, evolution].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.188 with low STS 0.298. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
